session.user: erk codespace connect. This is to change such that when it connects to the code space, it checks out the branch that is currently checked out on the local machine that we are conencting from

1→"""Connect to a codespace and launch Claude.""" 2→ 3→import shlex 4→ 5→import click 6→ 7→from erk.cli.commands.codespace.resolve import resolve_codespace 8→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 9→ 10→ 11→def _build_export_prefix(env_vars: tuple[str, ...]) -> str: 12→ """Parse --env KEY=VALUE args and return an export prefix string. 13→ 14→ Returns empty string if no env vars provided, otherwise returns 15→ 'export K1=V1 K2=V2 && ' ready to prepend to a command. 16→ """ 17→ if not env_vars: 18→ return "" 19→ 20→ parts: list[str] = [] 21→ for env_var in env_vars: 22→ if "=" not in env_var: 23→ click.echo(f"Error: Invalid --env format: {env_var!r} (expected KEY=VALUE)", err=True) 24→ raise SystemExit(1) 25→ key, value = env_var.split("=", 1) 26→ parts.append(f"{key}={shlex.quote(value)}") 27→ 28→ return f"export {' '.join(parts)} && " 29→ 30→
... [53 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Codespace execution abstraction for testing. 2→ 3→This module provides an ABC for codespace SSH operations to enable 4→testing without actually executing remote commands. 5→""" 6→ 7→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 8→from typing import NoReturn 9→ 10→ 11→class Codespace(ABC): 12→ """Abstract codespace SSH executor for dependency injection.""" 13→ 14→ @abstractmethod 15→ def exec_ssh_interactive(self, gh_name: str, remote_command: str) -> NoReturn: 16→ """Replace current process with SSH session to codespace. 17→ 18→ Uses os.execvp() to replace the current process, so this 19→ method never returns. 20→ 21→ Args: 22→ gh_name: GitHub codespace name (from gh codespace list) 23→ remote_command: Command to execute in the codespace 24→ 25→ Note: 26→ This method never returns - the process is replaced. 27→ """ 28→ ... 29→ 30→ @abstractmethod
... [26 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Unit tests for codespace connect command.""" 2→ 3→from datetime import datetime 4→ 5→from click.testing import CliRunner 6→ 7→from erk.cli.cli import cli 8→from erk.core.context import context_for_test 9→from erk_shared.context.types import LoadedConfig 10→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.fake import FakeCodespace 11→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.abc import RegisteredCodespace 12→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.fake import FakeCodespaceRegistry 13→ 14→ 15→def test_connect_shows_error_when_no_codespaces() -> None: 16→ """connect command shows error when no codespaces are registered.""" 17→ runner = CliRunner() 18→ 19→ codespace_registry = FakeCodespaceRegistry() 20→ ctx = context_for_test(codespace_registry=codespace_registry) 21→ 22→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "connect"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 23→ 24→ assert result.exit_code == 1 25→ assert "No default codespace set" in result.output 26→ assert "erk codespace setup" in result.output 27→ 28→ 29→def test_connect_shows_error_when_named_codespace_not_found() -> None: 30→ """connect command shows error when specified codespace doesn't exist."""
... [266 lines omitted] ...
37→ cli, ["codespace", "connect", "nonexistent"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False 45→def test_connect_shows_error_when_default_not_found() -> None: 46→ """connect command shows error when default codespace no longer exists.""" 61→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "connect"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 208→def test_connect_env_invalid_format_errors() -> None: 209→ """connect --env with invalid format (no =) shows error."""

1→"""Real Codespace implementation using gh codespace ssh. 2→ 3→RealCodespace provides SSH execution to GitHub Codespaces using 4→the gh CLI tool. 5→""" 6→ 7→import os 8→import subprocess 9→from typing import NoReturn 10→ 11→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.abc import Codespace 12→from erk_shared.subprocess_utils import run_subprocess_with_context 13→ 14→ 15→class RealCodespace(Codespace): 16→ """Production implementation using gh codespace ssh for remote execution.""" 17→ 18→ def start_codespace(self, gh_name: str) -> None: 19→ """Start a stopped codespace. 20→ 21→ Uses GitHub REST API POST /user/codespaces/{name}/start to ensure 22→ the codespace is running. 23→ 24→ Args: 25→ gh_name: GitHub codespace name (from gh codespace list) 26→ """ 27→ # GH-API-AUDIT: REST - codespace start 28→ run_subprocess_with_context( 29→ cmd=["gh", "api", "--method", "POST", f"/user/codespaces/{gh_name}/start"], 30→ operation_context=f"start codespace '{gh_name}'",

1→"""Fake Codespace implementation for testing. 2→ 3→FakeCodespace is an in-memory implementation that tracks SSH calls 4→without actually executing remote commands, enabling fast and deterministic tests. 5→""" 6→ 7→from dataclasses import dataclass 8→from typing import NoReturn 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.abc import Codespace 11→ 12→ 13→@dataclass(frozen=True) 14→class SSHCall: 15→ """Record of an SSH call for test assertions. 16→ 17→ Attributes: 18→ gh_name: GitHub codespace name that was targeted 19→ remote_command: Command that was executed 20→ interactive: Whether this was an interactive (exec) or non-interactive (run) call 21→ """ 22→ 23→ gh_name: str 24→ remote_command: str 25→ interactive: bool 26→ 27→ 28→class FakeCodespace(Codespace): 29→ """In-memory fake implementation that tracks SSH calls. 30→
... [101 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: I have a good understanding of the codebase now. The change is straightforward — inject a `git fetch && git checkout <branch>` into the remote command string. Let me clarify one design question.

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'Should the branch checkout happen as part of the setup command string (befo...[truncated 647 chars]...ssh_interactive. Better error handling but two SSH connections.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "Should the branch checkout happen as part of the setup command string (before `git pull && uv sync`), or should it be a separate `run_ssh_command` call before the interactive exec? The inline approach is simpler — it just adds `git fetch && git checkout <branch>` before `git pull`. The separate call approach would let us show an error message if checkout fails before launching Claude."="Inline in command string (Recommended)". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.

session.assistant: Now let me check `repo_root` property and how it works with `NoRepoSentinel`.
session.tool_result: 131: def repo_root(self) -> Path: 132- """Convenience property - get repo root from repo. 133- 134- Raises: 135- RuntimeError: If not in a git repository 136- """ 137- if isinstance(self.repo, NoRepoSentinel): 138- raise RuntimeError("Not in a git repository") 139- return self.repo.root 140- 141- @property
session.assistant:
Good — `repo_root` raises `RuntimeError` when there's no repo. But the connect command is only useful inside a repo, so this is fine. The existing tests use `NoRepoSentinel` by default though, so for tests that need `get_current_branch`, I'll need to pass a `repo` with a `RepoContext`.
Let me check `RepoContext` quickly.
session.tool_result: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py:103:class RepoContext: /Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-104- """Represents a git repo root and its managed worktrees directory. /Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-105- packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-106- Attributes: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-107- root: The actual working tree root (where git commands run). /Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-108- For worktrees, this is the worktree directory. /Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-109- For main repos, this equals main_repo_root. packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-110- repo_name: Name of the repository (derived from main_repo_root). packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-111- repo_dir: Path to erk metadata directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>). packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-112- worktrees_dir: Path to worktrees directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees). packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py-113- main_repo_root: The main repository root (for consistent metadata paths).

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/wobbly-sniffing-comet.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Codespace connect checks out local branch Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...

1→# Plan: Codespace connect checks out local branch 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→When running `erk codespace connect`, the remote codespace may be on a different branch than the local machine. The user wants the connect command to automatically check out the same branch that is currently checked out locally, so the codespace is immediately working on the right code. 6→ 7→## Approach 8→ 9→Inject `git fetch origin <branch> && git checkout <branch>` into the remote command string, before the existing `git pull` step. This keeps it as a single SSH connection. 10→ 11→Use `ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.repo_root)` to get the current local branch name. If `None` (detached HEAD), skip the checkout — proceed with existing behavior. 12→ 13→## Files to modify 14→ 15→### 1. `src/erk/cli/commands/codespace/connect_cmd.py` 16→ 17→After resolving the codespace (line 57), get the local branch and build a checkout prefix: 18→ 19→```python 20→local_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.repo_root) 21→if local_branch is not None: 22→ checkout_prefix = f"git fetch origin {shlex.quote(local_branch)} && git checkout {shlex.quote(local_branch)} && " 23→else: 24→ checkout_prefix = "" 25→``` 26→ 27→Insert `checkout_prefix` into the remote command in both paths: 28→ 29→- **Non-shell** (line 74): Change `setup` to `f"{checkout_prefix}git pull && uv sync && source .venv/bin/activate"` 30→- **Shell** (lines 69-72): Add `checkout_prefix` alongside `cd_prefix` and `export_prefix` in the `bash -l -c` command. The condition on line 69 becomes `if export_prefix or cd_prefix or checkout_prefix`.
... [28 lines omitted] ...
42→Existing tests pass `NoRepoSentinel` (the default) — they still work because `get_current_branch` returns `None` for unknown paths in FakeGit, so `checkout_prefix` will be `""`. **Wait** — actually `ctx.repo_root` will raise `RuntimeError` on `NoRepoSentinel`. Need to move the `get_current_branch` call to be guarded:
session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
---
# Plan: Codespace connect checks out local branch
## Context
When running `erk codespace connect`, the remote codespace may be on a different branch than the local machine. This change makes connect automatically check out the same branch locally checked out, so the codespace is immediately on the right code.
## Approach
Inject `git fetch origin <branch> && git checkout <branch>` into the remote command string before the existing `git pull` step. Single SSH connection, inline in the command string.
## Files to modify
### 1. `src/erk/cli/commands/codespace/connect_cmd.py` - Get local branch via `ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.repo_root)` (guarded for `NoRepoSentinel`) - Build `checkout_prefix` string, insert before `git pull` in both shell and non-shell paths
### 2. `tests/unit/cli/commands/codespace/test_connect_cmd.py` - 3 new tests: branch checkout, detached HEAD skip, shell+checkout
---

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Plan: Codespace connect checks out local branch\nCurrent context: (wt:erk...[truncated 375 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Plan: Codespace connect checks out local branch Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
Launching skill: erk:plan-save
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.

**Branch slug:** `codespace-connect-checkout-branch`
**Summary:** When connecting to a codespace, automatically check out the same git branch that is currently checked out on the local machine. This ensures the remote codespace is immediately working on the right code without manual branch switching.

tool_use.param:

## Context

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8900 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement plan #8900: In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout plan #8900: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8900 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8900
Dispatch plan #8900: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8900 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
Session complete. Do NOT call ExitPlanMode again.
session.assistant:
Plan #8900 saved. Here are your next steps:
**Implement plan #8900:** ``` In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8900 --script)" && erk implement -d ```
**Checkout plan #8900:** ``` In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8900 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8900 ```
**Dispatch plan #8900:** ``` CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8900 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch ```
session.user:
# /erk:pr-dispatch
## Goal
Find the most recent plan created in this conversation and dispatch it for remote AI implementation via `erk pr dispatch`.
## What This Command Does
1. Search conversation for the last plan reference 2. Extract the plan number 3. Run `erk pr dispatch <plan_number>` to trigger remote implementation
## Finding the Plan
Search the conversation from bottom to top for these patterns (in priority order):
1. **Draft PR reference**: `saved as draft PR #<number>` or `draft PR #<number>` 2. **Pull request URL**: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<number>` 3. **Plan URL**: `https://github.com/.../pull/<number>` (legacy: `https://github.com/.../issues/<number>`)
Extract the plan number from the most recent match.
## Execution
Once you have the plan number, run:
```bash erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session:
```bash erk exec marker delete --session-id "8d048b86-d41c-4312-a18c-2ded2cc63406" plan-saved-issue ```
Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state).
## Error Cases
- **No plan found in conversation**: Report "No plan found in conversation. Run /erk:plan-save first to create a plan." - **erk pr dispatch fails**: Display the error output from the command (erk pr dispatch validates the plan)
session.assistant: The most recent plan in this conversation is **#8900**. Dispatching it for remote implementation.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Dispatch plan #8900 for remote AI implementation

Dispatching workflow: plan-implement.yml Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow dispatched. ✓ Dispatch metadata written Posting queued event comment... ✓ Queued event comment posted
✓ 1 plan(s) dispatched successfully!

session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
